INFORMATION ON THE COMPANY </div></div></div> <div id="tx267971_10" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">A. History and Development of the Company </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-style: normal; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-style:italic;display:inline;">General facts on the company </div></div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Legal and commercial name of the Parent Company: </div></div>Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson (publ). </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Organization number: </div></div>556016-0680 </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Legal form of the Parent Company: </div></div>A Swedish limited liability company, organized under the Swedish Companies Act. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Country of incorporation: </div></div>Sweden. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Date of incorporation: </div></div>The Parent Company was incorporated on August&#160;18, 1918, as a result of a merger between AB LM Ericsson&#160;&amp; Co. and Stockholms Allm&#228;nna Telefon AB. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Domicile: </div></div>Our registered office is Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson, SE&#8211;164 83 Stockholm, Sweden. Our headquarters are located at Torshamnsgatan 21, Kista, Sweden. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Telephone number: </div></div>+46 10 719 0000 </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Website: </div></div><div style="text-decoration: underline; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">www.ericsson.com</div>. The information included on our website is not incorporated herein by reference. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">In addition, the SEC maintains an Internet site that contains reports, proxy and information statements, and other information regarding issuers that file electronically with the SEC: <div style="text-decoration: underline; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">http://www.sec.gov</div>. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Agent in the US: </div></div>Ericsson Inc., Vice President Legal Affairs, 6300 Legacy Drive, Plano, Texas 75024. Telephone number: +1 972 583 0000. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Shares: </div></div>Ericsson&#8217;s Class&#160;A and Class&#160;B shares are traded on Nasdaq Stockholm. In the US, our American Depository Shares (ADS), each representing one underlying Class&#160;B share, are traded on NASDAQ New York. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Parent company operations: </div></div>The business of the parent company, Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson, consists mainly of corporate management, holding company functions and internal banking activities. Our parent company operations also include customer credit management activities performed by Ericsson Credit AB on a commission basis. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;">Subsidiaries and associated companies: </div></div>For a list of our significant subsidiaries, please see &#8220;Item 4C. Shares owned directly by the Parent Company&#8221;. We are engaged in a number of minor joint ventures and cooperative arrangements. For more information regarding risks associated with joint ventures, strategic alliances and third-party agreements, please see &#8220;Item 3D. Financial Report &#8211; Risk Factors&#8221;. </div> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-style: normal; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-style:italic;display:inline;">Company history and development </div></div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="text-decoration: underline; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Innovating to empower people, business and society </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Our origins date back to 1876 when Alexander Graham Bell filed a patent application in the United States for the telephone. The same year, Lars Magnus Ericsson opened a small workshop in Stockholm to repair telegraph instruments and sell his own telephone equipment. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Today, Ericsson enables communications service providers to capture the full value of connectivity. The company&#8217;s portfolio spans Networks, Digital Services, Managed Services, and Emerging Business and is designed to help our customers go digital, increase efficiency and find new revenue streams. Ericsson&#8217;s investments in innovation have delivered the benefits of telephony and mobile broadband to billions of people around the world. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The information set forth under the following headings of the 2021 Swedish Annual Report (adjusted version) is incorporated herein by reference: </div></div> <div style="font-size: 6pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 6pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div>

<td style="vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;"> <div style="text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; line-height: normal;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Internal control risk </div></div> </td> </tr> </table> <div style="clear:both;max-height:0pt;"></div> <div style="font-size: 6pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 6pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div>
<table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border: 0px currentcolor; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; border-collapse: collapse; border-spacing: 0px;">

<td style="width: 9%;">&#160;</td>
<td style="width: 2%; vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width: 1%; vertical-align: top;">&#160;</td>
<td style="vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;"> <div style="text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; line-height: normal;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Environmental, social and governance risk </div></div> </td> </tr> </table> <div style="clear:both;max-height:0pt;"></div> <div style="font-size: 6pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 6pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div>
<table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border: 0px currentcolor; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; border-collapse: collapse; border-spacing: 0px;">
<tr style="page-break-inside: avoid;">
<td style="width: 6%;">&#160;</td>
<td style="width: 2%; vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width: 1%; vertical-align: top;">&#160;</td>
<td style="vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;"> <div style="text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; line-height: normal;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Corporate Governance Report </div></div> </td> </tr> </table> <div style="clear:both;max-height:0pt;"></div> <div style="font-size: 6pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 6pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div>
<table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border: 0px currentcolor; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; border-collapse: collapse; border-spacing: 0px;">
<tr style="page-break-inside: avoid;">
<td style="width: 9%;">&#160;</td>
<td style="width: 2%; vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width: 1%; vertical-align: top;">&#160;</td>
<td style="vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;"> <div style="text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; line-height: normal;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Regulation and compliance </div></div> </td> </tr> </table> <div style="clear:both;max-height:0pt;"></div> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-style: normal; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-style:italic;display:inline;">Disclosure pursuant to Section&#160;219 of the Iran Threat Reduction and Syria Human Rights Act of 2012 (ITRA) </div></div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Ericsson has conducted business in Iran/Persia since the late nineteenth century, opened an office in Iran in 1973 and later established a local subsidiary in the country. Ericsson strongly believes in enabling communication for all and believes that access to communications can enable the right to health, education and freedom of expression. Ericsson&#8217;s business activities in Iran principally involve the sale of communications infrastructure related products and services, including support, installation and maintenance services. Ericsson&#8217;s exports from the European Union (the &#8220;EU&#8221;) to Iran are performed under export licenses from the Swedish Inspectorate of Strategic Products and in compliance with applicable economic sanctions and export controls. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Due to its operations in Iran, and having staff permanently in the country, Ericsson has contacts with its local customers and retains certain local suppliers, including banks, and service providers. In addition, Ericsson has other dealings incidental to its local activities, such as making payments for taxes, customs related services, salaries, rents, utilities, local accommodation, transportation, office and similar supplies. As a result, Ericsson has contact with companies and public functions that may be owned or controlled by the government of Iran. While Ericsson seeks to obtain information regarding the actual business names and ownership of customers and other counterparties in Iran through its policies and procedures designed to ensure that Ericsson &#8220;knows its customers&#8221;, it is challenging to determine ownership and control with certainty, particularly with respect to determining whether an entity engaged in commercial activities is owned or controlled by the government. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Ericsson always strives to honor its engagements with existing customers in compliance with applicable export controls, sanctions and other laws, rules and regulations, carefully evaluates the implications of any sanctions and continues to monitor developments in this area as it relates to the ability to continue delivering products and services to customers. As a result of the US withdrawal from the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (the JCPOA) in 2018, Ericsson has reduced its business and organization in Iran significantly but still continues to provide critical services and support to its customers in order for their networks to function. As Ericsson provides such services and support, it may need to interact with other counterparties. Ericsson continues to explore, including with EU and US authorities, whether and how the last years&#8217; disruptive impact on the overall ability to support its customers can be reduced and, by doing so endeavor to avoid undue impacts on the access of the people of Iran to humanitarian items/basic services such as telecommunications. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">During 2021, Ericsson recorded revenues relating to the sale of communications infrastructure related products and services in Iran to the following telecommunications companies operating in the country: Mobile Communication Company of Iran (MCCI), and MTN Irancell. The revenues relating to MCCI relate to sales conducted before 2021. During 2021, Ericsson&#8217;s gross revenue (reported as net sales) related to sales to MCCI and MTN Irancell was approximately SEK 111&#160;million. In addition, Ericsson supplied network critical software capacities to MTN Irancell for a current value of approximately SEK 304 million during the year, which was not recorded as revenue as per the applied accounting policies, and thus Ericsson has not yet recorded any net revenues or net profits for these activities. Ericsson does not normally allocate net profit (reported as net income) on a <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">country-by-country</div></div> or <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">activity-by-activity</div></div> basis, other than as set forth in Ericsson&#8217;s consolidated financial statements prepared in accordance with IFRS as issued by the IASB. However, Ericsson has estimated that its operating income (income before taxes and financial net) from such sales was, after internal cost allocation approximately SEK 20&#160;million during 2021. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Ericsson uses the local banks Saman Bank and Pasargad Bank for local transactions in local currency, cash management and deposits. In some instances, Ericsson has had to arrange performance bonds or similar financial guarantees to secure Ericsson&#8217;s performance of obligations under the commercial agreements Ericsson has entered into relating to the business in Iran. In such instances, Ericsson usually engages its banks outside Iran, who in turn engage local banks in the country. These local banks include Tejarat Bank, Melli Bank, Parsian Bank, Saman Bank and Saderat Bank. Although some bonds and guarantees are still in place, no new performance bonds or similar guarantees involving these five banks with respect to Ericsson&#8217;s business activities in Iran were issued during 2021. During 2021, existing bank guarantees issued by Maskan Bank, Parsian Bank, Post Bank of Iran, Bank Mellat and Tejarat Bank (local banks in Iran) to secure Iranian customer payment obligations to Ericsson were renewed. Further, some payments made to Ericsson&#8217;s local subsidiary and payments required to be made by the local subsidiary to suppliers involve banks that may be controlled by the government of Iran. </div></div> <div id="tx267971_12" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">C. Organizational Structure </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">The following list shows certain shareholdings owned directly and indirectly by our parent company as of December&#160;31, 2021. A complete list of shareholdings, prepared in accordance with the Swedish Annual Accounts Act and filed with the Swedish Companies Registration Office (Bolagsverket), may be obtained upon request to: Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson, External Reporting, <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">SE-164</div> 83 Stockholm, Sweden. </div> <div style="font-size: 8pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 8pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div> <div style="text-align: center; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">3 </div></div> </div> </div> <div style="margin-top: 1em; margin-bottom: 0em; page-break-before: always;"></div> <hr style="width: 100%; height: 3px; color: rgb(153, 153, 153);"/> <div style="font-size: 13.28px; margin-top: 1.67em; margin-bottom: 1.67em;;font-weight: bold;"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div> <div style="text-align: center;"> <div style="width: 8.5in; text-align: left; margin-right: auto; margin-left: auto;"> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Shares owned directly by the Parent Company </div></div></div> <div style="font-size: 12pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 12pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div>

<td style="vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;"> <div style="text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; line-height: normal;">Five-year summary &#8211; Financial information - Statistical data, <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">year-end</div> </div> </td> </tr> </table> <div style="clear:both;max-height:0pt;"></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">See &#8220;Item 8.B. Financial Information &#8211; Significant Changes&#8221;, &#8220;Item 10.B. Memorandum and Articles of Association &#8211; Dividends&#8221; and &#8220;Item 17. Financial Statements&#8221;, herein. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Refer also to item 8.B herein </div></div> <div id="tx267971_34" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">B. Significant Changes </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Legal proceedings </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Ericsson and Apple were not able to renew the now expired patent license agreement between the parties in a timely manner. On January&#160;18, 2022, Ericsson filed three complaints with the U.S. International Trade Commission (ITC) alleging infringement of 12 patents by certain Apple products. In addition, Ericsson filed companion lawsuits in the Western District of Texas alleging infringement of the same 12 patents. Also, in January 2022 Ericsson filed complaints in several jurisdictions in Europe (Germany, Netherlands, Belgium) and South America (Brazil, Colombia) alleging that certain Apple products infringe Ericsson patents. On January&#160;19, 2022 Apple filed a complaint against Ericsson in the ITC alleging infringement of three Apple patents by certain Ericsson products. Apple also filed a complaint in Germany at the District court of D&#252;sseldorf alleging infringement of a German utility model and another complaint at the District court of Mannheim alleging infringement of an Apple patent by certain Ericsson products. The filing of lawsuits, </div></div> <div style="font-size: 8pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 8pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div> <div style="text-align: center; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">8 </div></div> </div> </div> <div style="margin-top: 1em; margin-bottom: 0em; page-break-before: always;"></div> <hr style="width: 100%; height: 3px; color: rgb(153, 153, 153);"/> <div style="font-size: 13.28px; margin-top: 1.67em; margin-bottom: 1.67em;;font-weight: bold;"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div> <div style="text-align: center;"> <div style="width: 8.5in; text-align: left; margin-right: auto; margin-left: auto;"> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">complaints and other proceedings, when parties take legal action over a patent license agreement renewal, is standard and consequently additional lawsuits, complaints and other proceedings, may follow. </div></div> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Euro Medium Term Note program </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">On February&#160;8, 2022, the Company issued new EUR 750&#160;million notes under the Euro Medium Term Note (EMTN) program, with maturity in February 2027. </div></div> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Vonage </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">In November 2021, Ericsson announced the entering into of an agreement to acquire Vonage Holdings Corp. for a total acquisition price of approximately USD 6.2&#160;billion. Since then, Vonage shareholder approval has been obtained and all requisite foreign and U.S. regulatory requirements for closing have been satisfied, except for receipt of clearance from the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States. If the agreement were to terminate under specified circumstances where we have failed to obtain such clearance, we may have to pay a USD 200&#160;million termination fee to Vonage. Ericsson and Vonage continue to work toward closing this acquisition during the first half of 2022, on and subject to the terms of the merger agreement and subject to receiving regulatory approval. </div></div> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Update on Deferred Prosecution Agreement </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">On December&#160;6, 2019, Ericsson entered into a Deferred Prosecution Agreement (DPA) with the United States Department of Justice (DOJ). On March&#160;1, 2022, the DOJ informed Ericsson that the disclosure made by the Company prior to the DPA about its internal investigation into conduct in Iraq in the period 2011 until 2019 was insufficient. Furthermore, it determined that the Company breached the DPA by failing to make subsequent disclosure related to the investigation <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">post-DPA.</div> The Company is in communication with the DOJ regarding the facts and circumstances of the breach determination and is committed to <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">co-operating</div> with the DOJ to resolve the matter. At this stage it is premature to predict the outcome of this matter. DOJ has sole discretion under the DPA to determine whether a breach has occurred. </div> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Legal proceedings </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">After the 2021 Swedish Annual Report was finalized on March&#160;3, 2022, Ericsson learned that Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson and certain officers of Ericsson were named as defendants in a putative class action filed in the United States District Court for the Eastern District of New York. The complaint alleges violations of United States securities laws, in connection with allegedly false and misleading statements principally concerning the Company&#8217;s adherence with its compliance and disclosure policies and obligations and the conduct of its business in Iraq. </div></div> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Ericsson appoints Scott Dresser as Chief Legal Officer </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">On March 16, 2022, Ericsson announced the appointment of Scott Dresser to the company&#8217;s Executive Team as Senior Vice President, Chief Legal Officer and Head of Group Function Legal Affairs&#160;&amp; Compliance. Scott has joined Ericsson on 21&#160;March 2022 and is based in the US. Scott Dresser replaces Xavier Dedullen, who resigned from this role as of the same date. </div></div> <div id="tx267971_35" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">ITEM&#160;9. THE OFFER AND LISTING </div></div></div> <div id="tx267971_36" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">A. Offer and Listing Details </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The information set forth in Exhibit 2.3, &#8220;Description of Securities Registered Pursuant to Section&#160;12 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.&#8221; is incorporated herein by reference. </div></div> <div id="tx267971_37" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">B. Plan of Distribution </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Not applicable. </div></div> <div id="tx267971_38" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">C. Markets </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The information set forth in Exhibit 2.3, &#8220;Description of Securities Registered Pursuant to Section&#160;12 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.&#8221; is incorporated herein by reference. </div></div> <div id="tx267971_39" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">D. Selling Shareholders </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Not applicable. </div></div> <div id="tx267971_40" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">E. Dilution </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Not applicable. </div></div> <div id="tx267971_41" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">F. Expenses of the Issue </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Not applicable. </div></div> <div id="tx267971_42" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">ITEM&#160;10. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION </div></div></div> <div id="tx267971_43" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">A. Share Capital </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Not applicable. </div></div> <div id="tx267971_44" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">B. Memorandum and Articles of Association </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The information set forth in Exhibit 2. 3, &#8220;Description of Securities Registered Pursuant to Section&#160;12 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.&#8221; is incorporated herein by reference. </div></div> <div id="tx267971_45" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">C. Material Contracts </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The information set forth under the following headings of the 2021 Swedish Annual Report (adjusted version) is incorporated herein by reference: </div></div> <div style="font-size: 6pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 6pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div>

<td style="width: 2%; vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width: 1%; vertical-align: top;">&#160;</td>
<td style="vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;"> <div style="text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; line-height: normal;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Board of Directors&#8217; report </div></div> </td> </tr> </table> <div style="clear:both;max-height:0pt;"></div> <div style="font-size: 6pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 6pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div>
<table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border: 0px currentcolor; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; border-collapse: collapse; border-spacing: 0px;">
<tr style="page-break-inside: avoid;">
<td style="width: 9%;">&#160;</td>
<td style="width: 2%; vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width: 1%; vertical-align: top;">&#160;</td>
<td style="vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;"> <div style="text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; line-height: normal;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Material contracts </div></div> </td> </tr> </table> <div style="clear:both;max-height:0pt;"></div> <div id="tx267971_46" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">D. Exchange Controls </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">There is no Swedish legislation affecting the import or export of capital or the remittance of dividends, interest or other payments to <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">non-resident</div> holders of our securities, except that, subject to the provisions in any tax treaty, dividends are subject to withholding tax. </div> <div style="font-size: 8pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 8pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div> <div style="text-align: center; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">9 </div></div> </div> </div> <div style="margin-top: 1em; margin-bottom: 0em; page-break-before: always;"></div> <hr style="width: 100%; height: 3px; color: rgb(153, 153, 153);"/> <div style="font-size: 13.28px; margin-top: 1.67em; margin-bottom: 1.67em;;font-weight: bold;"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div> <div style="text-align: center;"> <div style="width: 8.5in; text-align: left; margin-right: auto; margin-left: auto;"> <div id="tx267971_47" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">E. Taxation </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-style: normal; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-style:italic;display:inline;">General </div></div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The taxation discussion set forth below does not purport to be a complete analysis or listing of all potential tax effects relevant to the acquisition, ownership or disposition of Class&#160;B shares or ADSs. The statements of United States and Swedish tax laws set forth below are based on the laws in force as of the date of this report and may be subject to any changes in United States or Swedish law, and in any double taxation convention or treaty between the United States and Sweden, occurring after that date, which changes may then have retroactive effect. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Specific tax provisions may apply for certain categories of taxpayers. Your tax treatment if you are a holder of Class&#160;B shares or ADSs depends in part on your particular situation. If you are a holder of Class&#160;B shares or ADSs, you should therefore consult a tax advisor as to the tax consequences relating to your particular circumstances resulting from the ownership of Class&#160;B shares or ADSs. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The tax consequences to holders of ADSs, as discussed below, apply equally to holders of Class&#160;B shares. </div></div> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-style: normal; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-style:italic;display:inline;">Certain Swedish Tax Considerations </div></div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">This section describes the material Swedish income and net wealth tax consequences for a holder of ADSs or Class&#160;B shares who is not considered to be a Swedish resident for Swedish tax purposes. This section applies to you only if you are a holder of portfolio investments representing less than 10% of capital and votes and is not applicable if the ADSs or Class&#160;B shares pertain to a permanent establishment or fixed place of business in Sweden. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="text-decoration: underline; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Taxation on Capital Gains </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">Generally, <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">non-residents</div> of Sweden are not liable for Swedish capital gains taxation with respect to the sale of ADSs or Class&#160;B shares. However, under Swedish tax law, capital gains from the sale of shares in Swedish companies and certain other securities by an individual may be taxed in Sweden at a rate of 30% if the seller has been a resident of Sweden or has lived permanently in Sweden at any time during the year of the sale or the 10 calendar years preceding the year of the sale (absent treaty provisions to the contrary). The provision is applicable to ADSs or Class&#160;B shares. From January&#160;1, 2008, the rule has been extended so that it also applies to shares in foreign companies, provided that the shares were acquired during the time that the person was liable to tax in Sweden. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">This provision may, however, be limited by tax treaties that Sweden has concluded with other countries. Under the tax treaty between Sweden and the United States (the &#8220;U.S. Tax Treaty&#8221;), this provision applies for ten years from the date the individual became a <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">non-resident</div> of Sweden. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="text-decoration: underline; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Taxation on Dividends </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">A Swedish dividend withholding tax at a rate of 30% is imposed on dividends paid by a Swedish corporation, such as us, to <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">non-residents</div> of Sweden. The same withholding tax applies to certain other payments made by a Swedish corporation, including payments as a result of redemption of shares and repurchase of stock through an offer directed to its shareholders. Exemption from the withholding tax or a lower tax rate may apply by virtue of a tax treaty. Under the U.S. Tax Treaty, the withholding tax on dividends paid on portfolio investments to eligible U.S. holders is reduced to 15%. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">Under all Swedish tax treaties, except the tax treaty with Switzerland, withholding tax at the applicable treaty rate should be withheld by the payer of the dividends. With regard to dividends paid from shares in corporations registered with the Euroclear Sweden (such as our shares), a reduced rate of dividend withholding tax under a tax treaty is generally applied at the source by the Euroclear Sweden or, if the shares are registered with a nominee, the nominee, as long as the person entitled to the dividend is registered as a <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">non-resident</div> and sufficient information regarding the tax residency of the beneficial owner is available to the Euroclear Sweden or the nominee. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">In those cases where Swedish withholding tax is withheld at the rate of 30% and the person who received the dividends is entitled to a reduced rate of withholding tax under a tax treaty, a refund may be claimed from the Swedish tax authorities before the end of the fifth calendar year following the year that the distribution was made. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="text-decoration: underline; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Taxation on Interest </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">No Swedish withholding tax is payable on interest paid to <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">non-residents</div> of Sweden. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="text-decoration: underline; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Net Wealth Taxation </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The Swedish net wealth tax has been abolished from January&#160;1, 2007. </div></div> <div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-style: normal; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="font-style:italic;display:inline;">Certain United States Federal Income Tax Consequences </div></div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">The following discussion is a summary of the material United States federal income tax consequences relevant to the ownership and disposition of ADSs or Class&#160;B shares. This discussion is based on the tax laws of the United States (including the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended (the &#8220;Code&#8221;), its legislative history, existing and proposed regulations thereunder, published rulings and court decisions) as in effect on the date hereof, all of which are subject to change, possibly with retroactive effect. The discussion is not a full discussion of all tax considerations that may be relevant to the ownership and disposition of ADSs or Class&#160;B shares, and does not address the Medicare tax on net investment income or the effects of any state, local or foreign tax laws. The discussion applies only if you will hold the ADSs and/or the Class&#160;B shares as capital assets and you use the USD as your functional currency. It does not deal with the tax treatment of investors subject to special rules, such as grantor trusts, real estate investment trusts, regulated investment companies, banks, brokers or dealers in securities or currencies, traders in securities that elect to use a <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">mark-to-market</div></div> method of recording for their securities holdings, financial institutions, insurance companies, persons required to accelerate the recognition of any item of gross income with respect to our ADSs or Class&#160;B shares as a result of such income being recognized on an applicable financial statement, <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">tax-exempt</div> entities, investors liable for alternative minimum tax, holders (either actually or constructively) of 10% or more of the voting power or the value of our shares, persons holding ADSs and/or Class&#160;B shares as part of a hedging, straddle, conversion or constructive sale transaction and persons who are resident or ordinarily resident in Sweden. In addition, investors holding ADSs and/or Class&#160;B shares indirectly through partnerships are subject to special rules not discussed below. You should consult your own tax advisors about the United States federal, state, local and foreign tax consequences to you of the ownership and disposition of the ADSs or Class&#160;B shares. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The discussion below applies to you only if you are a beneficial owner of ADSs and/or Class&#160;B shares not resident in Sweden for purposes of the U.S. Tax Treaty and you are, for United States federal income tax purposes, (1)&#160;a citizen or resident of the United States, (2)&#160;a corporation or any other entity treated as a corporation that is organized in or under the laws of the United States or its political subdivisions, including the District of Columbia, (3)&#160;a trust if all of the trust&#8217;s substantial decisions are subject to the control of one or more United States persons and the primary supervision of the trust is subject to a United States court, or if a valid election is in effect with respect to the trust to be taxed as a United States person, or (4)&#160;an estate the income of which is subject to United States federal income taxation regardless of its source. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">The discussion below assumes that the representations contained in the deposit agreement governing the ADSs are true and that the obligations in the deposit agreement and any related agreement will be complied with in accordance with the terms. If you hold ADSs, you will be treated as the holder of the underlying Class&#160;B shares represented by those ADSs for United States federal income tax purposes. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="text-decoration: underline; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Dividends </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Subject to the passive foreign investment company rules discussed below, the gross amount of dividends paid (before reduction for any Swedish withholding taxes) with respect to the ADSs or Class&#160;B shares generally will be included in your gross income as ordinary income from foreign sources to </div></div> <div style="font-size: 8pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 8pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div> <div style="text-align: center; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">10 </div></div> </div> </div> <div style="margin-top: 1em; margin-bottom: 0em; page-break-before: always;"></div> <hr style="width: 100%; height: 3px; color: rgb(153, 153, 153);"/> <div style="font-size: 13.28px; margin-top: 1.67em; margin-bottom: 1.67em;;font-weight: bold;"><a href="#toc" style="text-indent: 0px;">Table of Contents</a></div> <div> <div style="text-align: center;"> <div style="width: 8.5in; text-align: left; margin-right: auto; margin-left: auto;"> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">the extent paid out of our current or accumulated earnings and profits (as determined for United States federal income tax purposes). Distributions in excess of earnings and profits will be treated as a <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">non-taxable</div> return of capital to the extent of your adjusted tax basis in the ADSs or Class&#160;B shares and thereafter as capital gain. The dividends will not be eligible for the dividends received deduction available to corporations in respect of dividends received from other U.S. corporations. The amount of any dividend paid in SEK will be the USD value of the dividend payment based on the exchange rate in effect on the date of receipt (or constructive receipt) by you, in the case of Class&#160;B shares, or by the depositary, in the case of ADSs, whether or not the payment is converted into USD at that time. Your tax basis in the SEK received will equal such USD amount. Gain or loss, if any, recognized on a subsequent sale or conversion of the SEK will be U.S. source ordinary income or loss. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">If you are a <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">non-corporate</div> holder of ADSs or Class&#160;B shares, dividends you receive on the ADSs or Class&#160;B shares may be taxed at the lower applicable long-term capital gains rate provided that (1)&#160;we are not a passive foreign investment company (as discussed below) for either our taxable year in which the dividend was paid or the preceding taxable year, (2)&#160;certain holding period requirements are met, (3)&#160;you are not under any obligation to make related payments with respect to substantially similar or related property and (4)&#160;either (a) in the case of ADSs our ADSs continue to be listed on the Nasdaq Stock Market (or a national securities exchange that is registered under section 6 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) or (b)&#160;we are eligible for the benefits of the U.S. Tax Treaty. You should consult your own tax advisors regarding the availability of the lower rate for dividends paid with respect to ADSs or Class&#160;B shares. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Subject to certain limitations, you will generally be entitled to receive credit against your United States federal income tax liability (or a deduction against your United States federal taxable income) with respect to any Swedish tax withheld in accordance with the U.S. Tax Treaty and paid over to Sweden. If a refund of the tax withheld is available to you under the laws of Sweden or under the U.S. Tax Treaty, the amount of tax withheld that is refundable will not be eligible for such credit against your United States federal income tax liability (and will not be eligible for the deduction in computing your United States federal taxable income). For foreign tax credit limitation purposes, dividends will be income from sources without the United States, and will generally be treated as &#8220;passive category income&#8221; (or, in the case of certain holders, &#8220;general category income&#8221;). </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="text-decoration: underline; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Sale or Exchange of ADSs or Class&#160;B shares </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">Subject to the passive foreign investment company rules discussed below, you will generally recognize capital gain or loss on the sale or other disposition of the ADSs or Class&#160;B shares equal to the difference between the USD value of the amount realized and your adjusted tax basis (determined in USD) in the ADSs or Class&#160;B shares. Such gain or loss will generally be long-term capital gain or loss if you have held the ADSs or Class&#160;B shares for more than one year, and will generally be treated as arising from U.S. sources for foreign tax credit limitation purposes. If you are a <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">non-corporate</div> holder of ADSs or Class&#160;B Shares, long-term capital gains are eligible for reduced rates of taxation. The deductibility of capital losses is subject to limitations. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">The amount realized on a disposition of ADSs or Class&#160;B shares for cash will generally be the amount of cash you receive for the ADSs or Class&#160;B shares (which, in the case of payment in a <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">non-U.S.</div> currency, will equal the USD value of the payment received determined on (a)&#160;the date of receipt of payment if you are a cash basis taxpayer and (b)&#160;the date of disposition if you are an accrual basis taxpayer). If the ADSs or Class&#160;B shares are treated as traded on an &#8220;established securities market&#8221; and you are a cash basis taxpayer (or, if you are an accrual basis taxpayer, if you so elect), you will determine the USD value of the amount realized by translating the amount received at the spot rate of exchange on the settlement date of the sale. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">You will have a tax basis in any foreign currency received equal to the USD value thereof on the date of receipt. Any gain or loss you realize on a subsequent sale or conversion of foreign currency will be U.S. source ordinary income or loss. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="text-decoration: underline; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Passive Foreign Investment Company Status </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">A <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">non-U.S.</div> corporation is a passive foreign investment company (a &#8220;PFIC&#8221;) in any taxable year in which, after taking into account the income and assets of certain subsidiaries, either (a)&#160;at least 75% of its gross income is passive income or (b)&#160;at least 50% of the quarterly average value of its assets is attributable to assets that produce or are held to produce passive income. Based on the market value of our shares, the composition of our assets and income and our operations, we believe we were not a PFIC during the year 2021. However, whether or not we will be considered a PFIC will depend on the nature and source of our income and the composition and value of our assets, as determined from time to time. If we are treated as a PFIC, we will not provide information necessary for the &#8220;qualified electing fund&#8221; election as the term is defined in the relevant provisions of the Code. You should consult your own tax advisors about the consequences of our potential classification as a PFIC. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">If we were classified as a PFIC with respect to your ADSs or Class&#160;B shares for any taxable year, we would generally continue to be a PFIC (unless certain conditions are met), and you would be subject to special rules with respect to: </div></div> <div style="font-size: 6pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 6pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div>

<table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border: 0px currentcolor; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; border-collapse: collapse; border-spacing: 0px;">

<td style="vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;"> <div style="text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; line-height: normal;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">the gain or any other excess distribution would be allocated ratably over your holding period for ADSs or Class&#160;B shares; </div></div> </td> </tr> </table> <div style="clear:both;max-height:0pt;"></div> <div style="font-size: 6pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 6pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div>
<table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border: 0px currentcolor; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; border-collapse: collapse; border-spacing: 0px;">

<td style="width: 1%; vertical-align: top;">&#160;</td>
<td style="vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;"> <div style="text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; line-height: normal;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">the amount allocated to the taxable year in which the gain or excess distribution was realized and any year before we became a PFIC would be taxable as ordinary income; </div></div> </td> </tr> </table> <div style="clear:both;max-height:0pt;"></div> <div style="font-size: 6pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 6pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div>
<table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border: 0px currentcolor; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; border-collapse: collapse; border-spacing: 0px;">
<tr style="page-break-inside: avoid;">
<td style="width: 3%;">&#160;</td>
<td style="width: 2%; vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width: 1%; vertical-align: top;">&#160;</td>
<td style="vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;"> <div style="text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; line-height: normal;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">the amount allocated to each prior year, other than the current year and any taxable year prior to the first taxable year in which we were a PFIC, would be subject to tax at the highest applicable marginal tax rate in effect for each such year; and </div></div> </td> </tr> </table> <div style="clear:both;max-height:0pt;"></div> <div style="font-size: 6pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 6pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div>
<table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border: 0px currentcolor; width: 100%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; border-collapse: collapse; border-spacing: 0px;">
<tr style="page-break-inside: avoid;">
<td style="width: 3%;">&#160;</td>
<td style="width: 2%; vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width: 1%; vertical-align: top;">&#160;</td>
<td style="vertical-align: top;;text-align:left;"> <div style="text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; line-height: normal;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">an interest charge would be imposed. </div></div> </td> </tr> </table> <div style="clear:both;max-height:0pt;"></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">If we are a PFIC for any taxable year, you will also be deemed to own shares in any of our subsidiaries that are also PFICs in such a year. As an alternative to the special rules described above, holders of &#8220;marketable stock&#8221; in a PFIC may elect <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">mark-to-market</div></div> treatment with respect to their ADSs or Class&#160;B shares. ADSs or Class&#160;B shares will not be considered marketable stock unless they are regularly traded on a qualified exchange or other market. If the <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">mark-to-market</div></div> election is available and you elect <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">mark-to-market</div></div> treatment you will, in general, include as ordinary income each year an amount equal to the increase in value of your ADSs or Class&#160;B shares for that year (measured at the close of your taxable year) and will generally be allowed a deduction for any decrease in the value of your ADSs or Class&#160;B shares for the year but only to the extent of previously included <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">mark-to-market</div></div> income. In addition, any gain you recognize upon the sale or other disposition of the ADSs or Class&#160;B shares will be treated as ordinary income and any loss will be treated as ordinary loss but only to the extent of previously included <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">mark-to-market</div></div> income. Any loss in excess of previously included <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">mark-to-market</div></div> income will be treated as a capital loss. However, a <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">mark-to-market</div></div> election would likely be unavailable with respect to your proportionate share in any of our subsidiaries that are PFICs. </div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">If you own ADSs or Class&#160;B shares during any year in which we are a PFIC, you will generally be required to make an annual return on IRS Form 8621. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="text-decoration: underline; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Information Reporting and Backup Withholding </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">In general, information reporting requirements will apply to dividends paid in respect of ADSs or Class&#160;B shares and the proceeds received on the sale or exchange of the ADSs or Class&#160;B shares within the United States or by a broker with certain United States connections. Backup withholding may apply to payments to you of dividends paid in respect of ADSs or Class&#160;B shares or the proceeds of a sale or other disposition of ADSs or Class&#160;B shares if you fail to provide an accurate taxpayer identification number (certified on IRS Form W&#8211;9) or, upon request, to certify that you are not subject to backup withholding or otherwise to comply with the applicable requirements of the backup withholding rules. The amount of any backup withholding from a </div></div> <div style="font-size: 8pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 8pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div> <div style="text-align: center; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">11 </div></div> </div> </div> <div style="margin-top: 1em; margin-bottom: 0em; page-break-before: always;"></div> <hr style="width: 100%; height: 3px; color: rgb(153, 153, 153);"/> <div style="font-size: 13.28px; margin-top: 1.67em; margin-bottom: 1.67em;;font-weight: bold;"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div> <div style="text-align: center;"> <div style="width: 8.5in; text-align: left; margin-right: auto; margin-left: auto;"> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">payment to you will be allowed as a credit against your United States federal income tax liability, and a refund of any excess amount withheld under the backup withholding rules may be obtained by filing the appropriate claim for refund with the Internal Revenue Service and furnishing any required information. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="text-decoration: underline; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Additional Reporting Requirements </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Certain holders who are individuals may be required to report information relating to an interest in ADSs or Class&#160;B shares, subject to certain exceptions (including an exception for ADSs or Class&#160;B shares held in accounts maintained by certain financial institutions). Holders should consult their tax advisors regarding the effects, if any, of these requirements on their ownership and disposition of ADSs or Class&#160;B shares. </div></div> <div id="tx267971_48" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">F. Dividends and Paying Agents </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Not applicable. </div></div> <div id="tx267971_49" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">G. Statement by Experts </div></div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">Not applicable. </div></div> <div id="tx267971_50" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">H. <td style="vertical-align: bottom;">&#160;&#160;</td>

<td style="vertical-align: bottom;">&#160;&#160;</td>

In 2021, such amount totaled approximately USD 11.6&#160;million. </div></div> <div style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">Effective January 2019, Deutsche Bank waived the cost of providing the ADS program administrative and reporting services to the extent provided by Deutsche Bank, and has agreed to bear the cost of certain third-party <div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;"><div style="white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">out-of-pocket</div></div> costs related to the ADS program up to USD 50,000 per year. These costs include costs for the local custodian&#8217;s administration of matters relating to meetings of shareholders and costs of certain transfer agent administration services, such as the registration and transfer of depositary receipts. In 2021, such amount totaled approximately USD 73,500. </div> <div style="font-size: 8pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-size: 8pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">&#160;</div></div> <div style="text-align: center; font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">13 </div></div> </div> </div> <div style="margin-top: 1em; margin-bottom: 0em; page-break-before: always;"></div> <hr style="width: 100%; height: 3px; color: rgb(153, 153, 153);"/> <div style="font-size: 13.28px; margin-top: 1.67em; margin-bottom: 1.67em;;font-weight: bold;"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div> <div style="text-align: center;"> <div style="width: 8.5in; text-align: left; margin-right: auto; margin-left: auto;"> <div id="tx267971_58" style="text-align: center; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">PART II </div></div></div> <div id="tx267971_59" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;"><div style="font-weight:bold;display:inline;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 9pt; letter-spacing: 0px; top: 0px;;display:inline;">ITEM&#160;13.
